owner__patient_information.reason_for_visit_today: Buddy has been intermittently vomiting small amounts of yellowish foamy liquid over the past three days, mostly early in the morning before breakfast. He also had two episodes of soft stool yesterday evening and this morning, and he seems a bit more tired than usual after our walks. I’m worried he might have eaten something in the backyard or at the dog park that upset his stomach. He has also been licking his lips more than usual and occasionally swallowing like he’s a bit nauseous. He did chew on a small piece of a rubber squeaky toy about a week ago, and while I thought I got all the pieces, I’m concerned there could be a small bit left in his stomach or intestines. I’d like him checked for dehydration, abdominal pain, and to see if any imaging or bloodwork is needed to rule out an obstruction or pancreatitis. He is still eating but a little more slowly than usual, and he turns his head away for a few seconds before finally deciding to eat. No obvious blood in vomit or stool that I’ve seen. I’m dropping him off today because I work a full day and want the doctor to be able to observe him, run tests, and call me with a plan, including whether he needs fluids, anti-nausea meds, or to stay overnight.
owner__patient_information.if_sick_for_how_long: Symptoms started about 3 days ago. First vomit episode was early morning three days ago, then once the next morning, and once this morning. Soft stools began yesterday evening and again this morning. Mild decrease in energy noticed over the last 2 days.
